natural_language: A retailer decides a single-period order quantity for one product. Demand is 111. Unit ordering cost is 1, holding cost for leftover inventory is 1, and shortage penalty is 9. Formulate and solve a linear optimization model.

A firm plans replenishment for one item over 4 periods. Initial inventory is 7. Period demands are [23, 21, 58, 49]. Unit ordering cost is 1 and holding cost is 4. Use inventory balance constraints to minimize total ordering and holding cost.

A firm plans replenishment for one item over 3 periods with backorders allowed. Initial inventory is 0. Period demands are [25, 33, 34]. Unit ordering cost is 5, holding cost is 1, and shortage penalty is 16. Formulate a linear model with inventory and shortage variables.

A warehouse replenishes 4 items over 3 periods. Initial inventories are [12, 3, 11, 11]. Demand matrix by item and period is [[44, 36, 24], [38, 47, 27], [10, 20, 37], [31, 27, 19]]. Item volumes are [2, 3, 1, 1] and storage capacity per period is 157. Minimize ordering and holding costs subject to inventory balance and capacity constraints.

A firm plans replenishment over 5 periods with fixed ordering cost. Initial inventory is 7. Demands are [51, 27, 60, 19, 17]. Unit ordering cost is 3, holding cost is 1, fixed ordering cost is 74. Use binary order trigger variables and Big-M constraints with M = 174.
